commitStrategyReview.notes: Lean Governance 2.0 implementation complete. Unified commands and Maintenance Mode verified locally.

units.purpose: reduce fragmented execution steps and automate staging/committing

summary: combine inspect, stage, and commit into a single atomic operation
remove legacy scaffold command and redundant manual steps
automate staging logic to reduce operational friction

units.purpose: allow unplanned high-quality fixes with minimal overhead

messagePreview.header: feat(governance): implement maintenance mode trailer support
summary: add Maintenance: true trailer to bypass plan-mapping requirements
update commitlint to recognize maintenance commits as valid
ensure pre-push validation honors the maintenance bypass signal

units.purpose: improve robustness of archival search and metadata tolerance

summary: implement recursive archival search to fix 'Plan not found' errors
relax lifecycle enforcement for completed plans in root during push
remove legacy aliases and redundant validation checks

units.purpose: align all documentation and workflows with Lean 2.0 architecture

summary: align core git-governance documentation with Lean 2.0 architecture
simplify plan-authoring and gatekeeper-commit workflow guides
document the new maintenance mode and unified command usage
